design_philosophy.core_loop: One-on-one fighting matches where players deplete opponent health bars through standard attacks, special moves, and Ki blast combos executed via button combinations
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time combat execution and frame-perfect special move timing against opponent AI or human player
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control iconic Dragon Ball Z characters in their various forms and power states, executing signature special moves and transformation sequences
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict adherence to Street Fighter II formula adapted for DBZ IP; emphasis on character roster and transformations over narrative progression; removal of story mode signals confidence in pure fighting game mechanics as primary engagement

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Versus and Tournament modes provide different challenge structures; character selection and transformation choices allow skill-based matchup navigation

progression.upgrade_system: Character transformations unlock through progression and gameplay; Ki gauge charging system provides resource management layer
progression.unlock_mechanism: Additional character unlockable via cheat code; transformations available to specific characters from roster of nine playable fighters
replayability_hooks: Nine character roster with distinct transformation states
Customizable six-button control scheme allows playstyle variation
Tournament mode with varied matchup progression

world_building_techniques: Character roster selection represents Majin Buu arc timeline
Character-specific movesets reference anime signature techniques

faction_indicators: Heroes: Goku, Goten, Trunks, Gohan, Supreme Kai, Android 18
Villains: Majin Vegeta, Majin Buu, Dabura
danger_signals: Majin Buu and Dabura represent major antagonistic forces from narrative arc
Majin Vegeta represents fallen hero turned dark power
environmental_storytelling: Tenkaichi Budōkai tournament setting grounds competitive framework in series lore
Split-screen mechanic maintains spatial coherence during DBZ's characteristic long-range combat exchanges

sources_used: wikipedia - Dragon Ball Z: Super Butōden 3
